topic: Sharp covariate-conditional identified set for the always-takers average dose-response function under a sufficient-set sample-selection restriction with continuous treatment (Lee-Liu 2025, the deferred sharp case): characterize it as the value of a global support-function / linear-fractional program over feasible conditional always-takers proportions pi_AT(X), coupled across X by the shared denominator E[pi_AT(X)] and solved via a scalar Dinkelbach/KKT threshold; with a primitive-verifiable condition C under which the pointwise-Frechet plug-in is exactly sharp; plus a doubly-robust kernel-localized estimator of the endpoints and inference over the identified set

open_problems.open_problem: Sharply characterize the covariate-conditional always-takers ADRF identified set under Lee-Liu sufficient-set selection by replacing pointwise Frechet pi_AT(X) plug-ins with a global denominator-coupled support-function program.

source_refs.claim: Generalizes Lee bounds to continuous or multivalued treatments and sufficient treatment values/sets for always-takers; the conditional sufficient-set sharp case leaves pi_AT(X) set-valued rather than point-identified.
source_refs.author: Ying-Ying Lee and Chu-An Liu

source_refs.claim: Distinguishes pointwise from uniform sharpness for covariate-conditional bounds and emphasizes that covariate averaging requires global compatibility conditions.
source_refs.author: Gustav Jonzon, Erin E. Gabriel, Arvid Sjolander, and Michael C. Sachs

source_refs.claim: Provides debiased estimation for bounds that are expectations of conditional LP optima, but does not cover global linear-fractional bounds coupled through E[pi_AT(X)].
source_refs.author: Eli Ben-Michael

source_refs.excerpt: law-level joint sharpness) is refuted by the density-version obstruction
Repairing this to field tier would require a different primitive

open_problems.why_unsolved: Lee-Liu identify the continuous-treatment always-takers target under sufficient treatment values, but in the sufficient-set case pi_AT(X) is only restricted to conditional Frechet intervals. The ADRF endpoint is a ratio with denominator E[pi_AT(X)], so choosing endpoint-favorable pi_AT(X) separately at each X need not be globally sharp. The prior dose-response-shape attempt records exactly this kind of failure: pointwise sharp continuous-dose fibers did not paste into a law-level sharp set without an additional primitive. The missing obstacle is a measurable-selection and global-compatibility theorem for the denominator-coupled feasible pi_AT(X) process, not another pointwise trimming formula.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem showing that every support-function direction equals a global linear-fractional program over measurable pi(X) in conditional Frechet intervals, plus a sharpness construction, a scalar Dinkelbach/KKT threshold characterization, and a primitive condition C under which the pointwise-Frechet plug-in satisfies all global KKT inequalities.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the direct anchor: continuous treatment, sample selection, always-takers ADRF, sufficient values/sets, covariates, and the deferred sharp sufficient-set case.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: Lee bounds handle binary-treatment sample selection -> Lee-Liu extend always-takers ADRFs to continuous treatment using sufficient values/sets -> sufficient-set covariate sharpness leaves pi_AT(X) Frechet-bounded and globally coupled

method_exemplars.technique: Support-function characterization of a Lee identified set with covariate-dependent monotonicity and orthogonal boundary moments.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It gives the closest Lee-bound support-function template, but with a point-identified always-takers covariate law rather than a denominator-coupled pi_AT(X) program.

method_exemplars.technique: Parametric transform for nonlinear fractional programs, turning ratio optimization into scalar root-finding over pointwise subproblems.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The Lee-Liu sufficient-set endpoint is a ratio with a shared denominator, so a Dinkelbach parameter is the natural object whose KKT inequalities define the sharp pi_AT(X) selector.

method_exemplars.technique: Pointwise-versus-uniform sharpness taxonomy and compatibility arguments for covariate-conditional bounds.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The proposed condition C is exactly a uniform/global sharpness condition for aggregating covariate-conditional Frechet fibers.

open_problems.open_problem: Develop valid endpoint estimation and inference for the Lee-Liu sufficient-set ADRF once its sharp endpoint is computed by a kernel-localized, thresholded, denominator-coupled program.

source_refs.claim: Kernel-localized double/debiased ML gives nonparametric-rate inference for point-identified continuous-treatment ADRFs, but not for nonregular partial-ID endpoints with estimated active sets.
source_refs.author: Kyle Colangelo and Ying-Ying Lee

source_refs.claim: Debiases conditional LP bounds and proves asymptotic normality, but the Lee-Liu endpoint is a global fractional threshold problem rather than an expectation of independent conditional LP solutions.
source_refs.author: Eli Ben-Michael

source_refs.excerpt: The same-class UPPER endpoint remains an open residual
no endpoint limit law or CI theory is derived

open_problems.why_unsolved: The estimator is not a standard continuous-treatment ADRF DML problem: the target endpoint depends on conditional selection probabilities, conditional quantiles/trimmed moments, an estimated density-localized dose, and a scalar threshold whose active-set geometry can change under local perturbations. Existing continuous-treatment DML handles point-identified smooth ADRFs; conditional-LP inference handles expectation-of-LP bounds; prior CausalSmith stat gaps warn that localized upper endpoints and boundary-irregular partial-ID endpoints need extra smoothness and directional-derivative theory. The missing obstacle is the limit law and honest coverage for a thresholded support-function endpoint under kernel localization and estimated nuisance functions.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A cross-fitted kernel-localized estimator of each sharp endpoint, with a Neyman-orthogonal or debiased score for the conditional trimmed moments, a directional-derivative limit law for the Dinkelbach threshold map, and confidence sets with valid coverage across regular and active-set-boundary regimes.

writing_exemplar.why: Its setup cleanly motivates kernel localization and doubly robust continuous-treatment inference before the proposal pivots to partial-ID endpoint nonregularity.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: Continuous treatments require local dose-response inference -> kernel-localized DML removes first-order nuisance bias for point-identified ADRFs -> sharp Lee-Liu endpoints require the same localization inside a nonsmooth fractional support-function map

method_exemplars.technique: Kernel-localized doubly robust score with cross-fitting and nonparametric-rate asymptotic normality.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The Lee-Liu endpoint estimator uses the same dose-localization nuisance structure before applying the partial-ID support-function map.

method_exemplars.technique: LP-solver-based debiasing and asymptotic normality for conditional partial-ID bound functionals.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its debiasing strategy is structurally closest to partial-ID bounds with estimated conditional objectives and constraints, even though the target here is fractional and globally coupled.

method_exemplars.technique: Directional delta method and resampling for nonsmooth functionals where the usual bootstrap fails.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The Dinkelbach endpoint and active-set map are directionally differentiable rather than fully smooth at threshold ties.

open_problems.open_problem: Compute an explicit active-constraint threshold for a finite adjacent-rank continuous-dose IV/copula LP, as a reusable certificate for denominator-coupled sharp bounds that are not pointwise separable.

source_refs.excerpt: Conjecture 2: two-bin copula-radius phase transition (NOT YET PROVED)
critical radius as an LP active-constraint threshold or a minimum over finitely many affine inequalities

open_problems.why_unsolved: The accepted dose-response IV proposal confirmed a witness after derivation, but its reviewers explicitly identified the stronger open algebraic target: the phase boundary should be a minimum over active affine inequalities rather than an existential LP crossing. That is the finite-dimensional analogue of the Lee-Liu Dinkelbach/KKT threshold problem: a scalar threshold encodes which conditional cells select boundary pi values, and the proof must show that the threshold is primitive and auditable rather than a numerical solver artifact.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An explicit finite LP theorem giving the endpoint as a piecewise-affine support function and the critical scalar threshold as a minimum over active constraints, with a proof that the threshold formula is stable under small perturbations of the observed margins.

writing_exemplar.why: Its motivation arc explains why extra copula information can turn marginally ambiguous potential-outcome bounds into sharper joint-distribution restrictions.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: Marginal potential-outcome distributions leave treatment-effect functionals weakly bounded -> copula restrictions encode dependence information -> finite LP active constraints can certify a sharp threshold

method_exemplars.technique: Copula-restriction partial identification through finite-dimensional joint-distribution constraints.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The CausalSmith gap asks for the exact finite LP boundary generated by copula-tube restrictions.

method_exemplars.technique: Separation of pointwise Makarov-type sharpness from joint/uniform sharpness for treatment-effect distribution bounds.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the closest warning that pointwise sharp bounds do not automatically solve a global threshold problem.

method_exemplars.technique: Calibrated projection confidence intervals that repeatedly solve constrained optimization programs for partially identified parameters.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Once the active-threshold LP is stated, inference naturally reduces to calibrated optimization over a finite partially identified set.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive sharp principal-stratum linear-fractional endpoint programs for always-taker/complier mixture deconvolution when retained assignment memory changes the feasible response-type polytope.

source_refs.excerpt: first-period always-taker and complier strata both have positive mass
minimum and maximum of the linear-fractional target

open_problems.why_unsolved: The archived angle was abandoned in favor of a narrower dynamic-compliance kernel, but it isolates the same structural obstacle as the Lee-Liu sufficient-set problem: the target averages over a latent principal stratum whose mass is itself an unknown denominator. Retaining assignment memory changes the feasible response-type polytope, so simply plugging in cellwise stratum proportions may miss cross-cell compatibility restrictions. The open part is not the existence of a response-type LP, but the sharp fractional endpoint and the conditions under which coarsening or Markovization leaves it unchanged.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite response-type LP or Charnes-Cooper transformed program for the sharp endpoint, together with necessary-and-sufficient conditions for strict memory gain versus equality with the coarsened Markovized identified set.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the canonical template for translating principal strata and imperfect compliance into sharp finite response-type bounds.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: Imperfect compliance creates latent principal strata -> response-type enumeration converts causal restrictions into linear feasibility -> always-taker/complier mixtures with unknown denominators require sharp fractional endpoint programs

method_exemplars.technique: Finite response-type enumeration and linear-programming sharp bounds for instrumental-variable compliance models.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The archived angle is explicitly a principal-strata response-type feasibility problem with a fractional target.

method_exemplars.technique: Scalar fractional-programming transform for optimization with an unknown positive denominator.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The endpoint is a ratio over an unknown principal-stratum mass, matching the same denominator obstacle as the always-takers ADRF problem.

method_exemplars.technique: Power comparison and construction of confidence intervals for interval-identified parameters with ordered bound estimators.
method_exemplars.why_closest: After the sharp fractional endpoints are derived, the resulting interval estimand fits the ordered-bound inference setting.

literature_map:
Lee (2009) is the binary-treatment sample-selection anchor: monotonicity identifies the always-taker share and sharp trimming bounds follow.
Semenova (2024) generalizes Lee bounds with covariates, support-function inference, and orthogonal moments, but still in a setting where the relevant always-taker covariate law is identified.
Lee and Liu (2025) move to continuous or multivalued treatments, sufficient treatment values, and sufficient sets for always-takers.
In Lee-Liu's sufficient-value case pi_AT(X) is identified by a conditional minimum selection probability; in the sufficient-set case it is only Frechet-bounded.
The deferred sharp problem is global because the always-taker covariate law is weighted by the shared denominator E[pi_AT(X)].
Jonzon, Gabriel, Sjolander, and Sachs (2025) make the same conceptual distinction between pointwise and uniform sharpness for covariate-conditional bounds.
Ben-Michael (2025) gives debiased estimation for conditional LP bounds, but those bounds aggregate independent conditional LP optima rather than a denominator-coupled fractional program.
Dinkelbach (1967) supplies the scalar fractional-programming transform that naturally yields the KKT threshold for pi_AT(X).
Colangelo and Lee (2025) and Kennedy, Ma, McHugh, and Small (2017) supply kernel-localized doubly robust continuous-treatment inference for point-identified ADRFs.
Fang and Santos (2019) and Kaido, Molinari, and Stoye (2019) are method exemplars for nonsmooth partial-ID endpoint inference once active sets and support functions enter.
Adjacent Lee-bound work with richer latent strata reinforces that mixture weights often require global optimization rather than pointwise plug-in logic.
No located paper appears to give the Lee-Liu sufficient-set sharp covariate-conditional always-takers ADRF program, its primitive plug-in sharpness condition, or endpoint inference.
prior_proposal_map:
Direct qid pid_alwaystaker_adrf_sharp_conditional currently has only initialization state/pipeline artifacts; no local proposal or reviews exist yet.
pid_dose_response_shape_v1 is the main warning source: reviewers and pipeline notes say law-level joint sharpness failed because pointwise continuous-dose fibers did not paste.
pid_dose_response_iv_v1 supplies a reviewer-prescribed finite active-threshold problem: a two-bin copula-radius phase transition should be stated as an LP active-constraint minimum.
pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2 angle0_rejected is the closest explicit always-taker/principal-strata fractional-program artifact, with nonzero always-taker and complier strata and Charnes-Cooper language.
stat_dose_response_minimax_holder_anisotropic_converse supplies the localized DR upper-endpoint residual and cautions that slice smoothness is insufficient.
stat_prte_ot_boundary_adaptation proto_core supplies a partial-ID endpoint-inference analogue: no endpoint limit law or honest CI was derived.
Useful misses: no mined prior file names Lee-Liu 2025 or the sufficient-set continuous-treatment always-taker ADRF directly.
Filtered out: broader continuous-IV support-function path-compatibility gaps were too far from the always-taker sample-selection substrate for this run.
